title: trifolium kingii subsp. dedeckerae
intro: Trifolium kingii subsp. dedeckerae, also known as King's clover or Dedekera's clover, is a rare sub-species of the Trifolium kingii plant, a type of legume native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of isoflavones, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Trifolium kingii subsp. dedeckerae include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as providing potential relief from menopausal symptoms and osteoporosis. Trifolium kingii subsp. dedeckerae can be prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
